title: A resolution acknowledging the third anniversary of Russia's further invasion of Ukraine and expressing support for the people of Ukraine.
plain_summary: This bill is about supporting Ukraine on the third anniversary of when Russia attacked the country. The US Senate wants to show they are on Ukraine's side and think Russia was wrong to invade. The bill says America will keep helping Ukraine stay independent and make its own decisions as a country. It strongly disagrees with what Russia did and wants Ukraine to be free.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S1314)

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This is a resolution that recognizes the third anniversary of when Russia invaded Ukraine again and shows support for the Ukrainian people.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: On February twenty-fourth, two thousand twenty-two, Russia started a large-scale attack on Ukraine without any good reason. This attack was against the law under international rules. This was not the first time Russia attacked Ukraine. In two thousand fourteen, Russia illegally took over a part of Ukraine called Crimea and also illegally occupied parts of the Donbas region. Countries around the world agree that Ukraine is a sovereign nation (meaning it has the right to govern itself) and that its borders from nineteen ninety-one should be respected. The Ukrainian military and Ukrainian people have fought back strongly and have stopped Russia from taking over their country.

sections.title: What the Senate Decides
sections.text: The Senate declares that it stands with the Ukrainian people and feels sad about the tens of thousands of Ukrainians who have died because of Russian attacks. The Senate rejects Russia's attempts to take over Ukrainian territory by force and refuses to accept similar actions anywhere else in Europe. The Senate confirms that the United States supports Ukraine's right to govern itself and control its own territory. The Senate praises NATO (a military alliance), the Ukraine Defense Contact Group (a group that helps Ukraine defend itself), and other countries for continuing to help Ukraine defend itself and protect human rights. The Senate supports Ukraine's goal to join European and Atlantic organizations. The Senate recognizes that Ukraine is working to make its democracy stronger even during wartime. The Senate encourages America's allies to keep condemning Russia's illegal war against Ukraine and to fight against Russian aggression. The Senate emphasizes that Ukraine must be included in any talks with Russia about Ukraine's future.
